metadata.title: Random Variables and Hypothesis Testing
learning_objectives: Work with Bernoulli and binomial distributions
Visualize probability distributions and rejection regions
Perform one-tailed and two-tailed hypothesis tests

snippets.title: Setup and Imports
snippets.description: Import necessary libraries for probability and statistics calculations
snippets.code:
# Week 10: Hypothesis Testing - Setup
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from scipy.stats import binom, binomtest
import pandas as pd

# Set display options
pd.set_option('display.max_rows', None)
np.set_printoptions(precision=5)

print("Libraries loaded successfully!")
snippets.expected_output: Confirmation message that libraries are loaded

snippets.title: Expected Value Calculation
snippets.description: Calculate expected value from a probability distribution table (Q33 style)
snippets.code:
# Expected Value Calculation (Q33 Style)
# Given probability distribution:
# X:    0    3    4    6
# P(X): 0.2  0.4  0.3  0.1

x_values = np.array([0, 3, 4, 6])
probabilities = np.array([0.2, 0.4, 0.3, 0.1])

# Verify probabilities sum to 1
print(f"Sum of probabilities: {probabilities.sum()}")

# Calculate E[X]
expected_value = np.sum(x_values * probabilities)
print(f"\nE[X] = {x_values[0]}×{probabilities[0]} + {x_values[1]}×{probabilities[1]} + {x_values[2]}×{probabilities[2]} + {x_values[3]}×{probabilities[3]}")
print(f"E[X] = {x_values[0]*probabilities[0]} + {x_values[1]*probabilities[1]} + {x_values[2]*probabilities[2]} + {x_values[3]*probabilities[3]}")
print(f"E[X] = {expected_value}")

# Calculate E[X²] and Variance
expected_x_squared = np.sum(x_values**2 * probabilities)
variance = expected_x_squared - expected_value**2
std_dev = np.sqrt(variance)

print(f"\nE[X²] = {expected_x_squared}")
print(f"Var(X) = E[X²] - (E[X])² = {expected_x_squared} - {expected_value}² = {variance}")
print(f"Standard deviation = {std_dev:.4f}")

snippets.title: Bernoulli Distribution
snippets.description: Work with Bernoulli distribution for single trial experiments
snippets.code:
# Bernoulli Distribution: Single trial with two outcomes
# Example: Single contact transmission probability

p = 0.6  # Probability of transmission

# Bernoulli is a special case of Binomial with n=1
# P(X=0) = 1-p, P(X=1) = p

print("Bernoulli Distribution (p = 0.6)")
print("="*40)
print(f"P(X = 0) = 1 - p = {1-p}")
print(f"P(X = 1) = p = {p}")

# Mean and Variance
mean_bernoulli = p
var_bernoulli = p * (1 - p)

print(f"\nE[X] = p = {mean_bernoulli}")
print(f"Var(X) = p(1-p) = {p}×{1-p} = {var_bernoulli}")
print(f"Standard deviation = {np.sqrt(var_bernoulli):.4f}")

# Visualize
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 4))
ax.bar([0, 1], [1-p, p], color=['steelblue', 'coral'], edgecolor='black')
ax.set_xlabel('X (0 = No transmission, 1 = Transmission)')
ax.set_ylabel('Probability')
ax.set_title(f'Bernoulli Distribution (p = {p})')
ax.set_xticks([0, 1])
ax.set_ylim(0, 1)
for i, v in enumerate([1-p, p]):
    ax.text(i, v + 0.02, f'{v:.2f}', ha='center', fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
snippets.expected_output: Bernoulli distribution parameters and bar chart visualization

snippets.title: Binomial Distribution - PMF Calculation
snippets.code:
# Binomial Distribution: Number of successes in n trials
# Example: Number of infections out of 12 contacts

n = 12  # number of trials
p = 0.5  # probability of success per trial

print(f"Binomial Distribution: X ~ Binomial({n}, {p})")
print("="*50)

# Calculate probabilities for all possible outcomes
k_values = np.arange(0, n + 1)
probabilities = binom.pmf(k_values, n, p)

# Create a nice table
df = pd.DataFrame({
    'k (successes)': k_values,
    'P(X = k)': probabilities,
    'P(X = k) rounded': np.round(probabilities, 5)
})
print(df.to_string(index=False))

# Mean and Variance
mean_binom = n * p
var_binom = n * p * (1 - p)

print(f"\nE[X] = np = {n}×{p} = {mean_binom}")
print(f"Var(X) = np(1-p) = {n}×{p}×{1-p} = {var_binom}")
print(f"Standard deviation = {np.sqrt(var_binom):.4f}")
snippets.expected_output: Table of binomial probabilities for all k values

snippets.title: Visualizing Binomial Distribution
snippets.description: Create bar chart of binomial distribution with mean marked
snippets.code:
# Visualize Binomial Distribution
n = 12
p = 0.5

k_values = np.arange(0, n + 1)
probabilities = binom.pmf(k_values, n, p)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
bars = ax.bar(k_values, probabilities, color='steelblue', edgecolor='black', alpha=0.7)

# Mark the mean
mean = n * p
ax.axvline(x=mean, color='red', linestyle='--', linewidth=2, label=f'Mean = {mean}')

# Add labels for probabilities
for k, prob in zip(k_values, probabilities):
    if prob > 0.01:  # Only label visible bars
        ax.text(k, prob + 0.005, f'{prob:.3f}', ha='center', fontsize=8)

ax.set_xlabel('k (Number of Successes)', fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel('P(X = k)', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title(f'Binomial Distribution: X ~ Binomial({n}, {p})', fontsize=14)
ax.set_xticks(k_values)
ax.legend()
ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
snippets.expected_output: Bar chart showing binomial probabilities with mean marked

snippets.title: p-Value Calculation Using binom.pmf
snippets.description: Calculate p-value manually by summing probabilities (understanding the concept)
snippets.code:
# p-Value Calculation: Manual method using binom.pmf
# Scenario: Testing if coin is fair after observing 10 heads in 12 tosses

n = 12
p_null = 0.5  # Null hypothesis: fair coin
observed_k = 10  # Observed successes

print("Calculating p-value for One-Tailed Test (Upper)")
print("H₀: p ≤ 0.5 vs Hₐ: p > 0.5")
print(f"Observed: {observed_k} successes out of {n} trials")
print("="*50)

# Calculate probabilities for k = 10, 11, 12
print("\nProbabilities for extreme values (k ≥ 10):")
for k in range(observed_k, n + 1):
    prob = binom.pmf(k, n, p_null)
    print(f"P(X = {k}) = {prob:.5f}")

# p-value = P(X ≥ observed_k | H₀)
p_value_upper = sum(binom.pmf(k, n, p_null) for k in range(observed_k, n + 1))
print(f"\np-value (one-tailed, upper) = P(X ≥ {observed_k}) = {p_value_upper:.5f}")

# For two-tailed test, include equally extreme values from lower tail
# Under H₀: p=0.5, the distribution is symmetric
# k=10 deviates by 4 from mean=6, so include k=2, 1, 0 as well
print("\nFor Two-Tailed Test:")
lower_extreme = n - observed_k  # Symmetric point: 12 - 10 = 2
p_value_lower = sum(binom.pmf(k, n, p_null) for k in range(0, lower_extreme + 1))
p_value_two_tailed = p_value_upper + p_value_lower

print(f"P(X ≤ {lower_extreme}) = {p_value_lower:.5f}")
print(f"p-value (two-tailed) = {p_value_two_tailed:.5f}")
snippets.expected_output: Step-by-step p-value calculation for both one-tailed and two-tailed tests

snippets.title: p-Value Using binomtest Function
snippets.description: Use scipy's binomtest for quick hypothesis testing
snippets.code:
# p-Value Calculation Using binomtest (Recommended Method)
from scipy.stats import binomtest

n = 12
observed_k = 10
p_null = 0.5

print("Using scipy.stats.binomtest()")
print("="*50)

# One-tailed test (upper): Hₐ: p > p₀
result_greater = binomtest(k=observed_k, n=n, p=p_null, alternative='greater')
print(f"\nOne-tailed test (Hₐ: p > 0.5):")
print(f"  Observed proportion: {result_greater.proportion_estimate:.4f}")
print(f"  p-value = {result_greater.pvalue:.5f}")

# One-tailed test (lower): Hₐ: p < p₀
result_less = binomtest(k=observed_k, n=n, p=p_null, alternative='less')
print(f"\nOne-tailed test (Hₐ: p < 0.5):")
print(f"  p-value = {result_less.pvalue:.5f}")

# Two-tailed test: Hₐ: p ≠ p₀
result_two_sided = binomtest(k=observed_k, n=n, p=p_null, alternative='two-sided')
print(f"\nTwo-tailed test (Hₐ: p ≠ 0.5):")
print(f"  p-value = {result_two_sided.pvalue:.5f}")

# Decision at α = 0.05
alpha = 0.05
print(f"\nDecision at α = {alpha}:")
print(f"  One-tailed (upper): {'Reject H₀' if result_greater.pvalue < alpha else 'Fail to reject H₀'}")
print(f"  Two-tailed: {'Reject H₀' if result_two_sided.pvalue < alpha else 'Fail to reject H₀'}")
snippets.expected_output: Results from binomtest for different alternative hypotheses

snippets.title: Q35 Exam Question - Complete Solution
snippets.description: Solve the exact exam question about virus variant transmissibility
snippets.code:
# Q35 Exam Question: New Virus Variant Transmissibility
print("Q35: Is the new variant more infectious?")
print("="*60)

# Given data
n = 11  # Number of contacts
observed_infections = 9  # Observed infections
p_old = 0.50  # Old variant infection rate

print(f"\nData: {observed_infections} infections out of {n} contacts")
print(f"Old variant rate: p = {p_old}")
print(f"Question: Is the new variant MORE infectious?")

# CORRECT approach: One-tailed test
print("\n" + "-"*60)
print("CORRECT APPROACH: One-tailed test")
print("-"*60)
print("H₀: p ≤ 0.50 (new variant is NOT more infectious)")
print("Hₐ: p > 0.50 (new variant IS more infectious)")

result_one_tailed = binomtest(k=observed_infections, n=n, p=p_old, alternative='greater')
print(f"\np-value = P(X ≥ {observed_infections} | p = 0.5) = {result_one_tailed.pvalue:.5f}")
print(f"         = 67/2048 ≈ 0.033")
print(f"\nSince {result_one_tailed.pvalue:.3f} < 0.05, REJECT H₀")
print("Conclusion: Evidence supports that new variant is more infectious")

# INCORRECT approach: Two-tailed test
print("\n" + "-"*60)
print("INCORRECT APPROACH: Two-tailed test")
print("-"*60)
print("H₀: p = 0.50")
print("Hₐ: p ≠ 0.50")

result_two_tailed = binomtest(k=observed_infections, n=n, p=p_old, alternative='two-sided')
print(f"\np-value = {result_two_tailed.pvalue:.5f}")
print(f"\nSince {result_two_tailed.pvalue:.3f} > 0.05, FAIL TO REJECT H₀")
print("This would lead to the WRONG conclusion for this question!")

print("\n" + "="*60)
print("KEY INSIGHT: Match the test type to the research question!")
print("'More infectious' → One-tailed (upper) test")
print("="*60)
snippets.expected_output: Complete solution showing why one-tailed test is correct for Q35

snippets.title: Visualizing p-Values and Rejection Regions
snippets.description: Create visualization showing the rejection region and p-value
snippets.code:
# Visualize p-Value and Rejection Region
n = 11
p_null = 0.5
observed_k = 9
alpha = 0.05

k_values = np.arange(0, n + 1)
probabilities = binom.pmf(k_values, n, p_null)

# Determine colors: rejection region (p-value region) in red
colors = ['coral' if k >= observed_k else 'steelblue' for k in k_values]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 6))
bars = ax.bar(k_values, probabilities, color=colors, edgecolor='black', alpha=0.7)

# Add horizontal line at the probability level of observed value
prob_observed = binom.pmf(observed_k, n, p_null)
ax.axhline(y=prob_observed, color='red', linestyle='--', alpha=0.5)

# Calculate and display p-value
p_value = sum(probabilities[k_values >= observed_k])

# Add annotations
ax.annotate(f'Observed: k = {observed_k}', xy=(observed_k, prob_observed),
            xytext=(observed_k + 1.5, prob_observed + 0.05),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='red'),
            fontsize=11, color='red', fontweight='bold')

ax.text(0.02, 0.95, f'p-value = P(X ≥ {observed_k}) = {p_value:.4f}',
        transform=ax.transAxes, fontsize=12, verticalalignment='top',
        bbox=dict(boxstyle='round', facecolor='lightyellow', alpha=0.8))

ax.text(0.02, 0.85, f'α = {alpha}',
        transform=ax.transAxes, fontsize=12, verticalalignment='top',
        bbox=dict(boxstyle='round', facecolor='lightgreen', alpha=0.8))

decision = 'Reject H₀' if p_value < alpha else 'Fail to reject H₀'
ax.text(0.02, 0.75, f'Decision: {decision}',
        transform=ax.transAxes, fontsize=12, verticalalignment='top',
        bbox=dict(boxstyle='round', facecolor='lightcoral' if p_value < alpha else 'lightgray', alpha=0.8))

ax.set_xlabel('k (Number of Infections)', fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel('P(X = k) under H₀: p = 0.5', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title(f'One-Tailed Test: P(X ≥ {observed_k}) under H₀\n(Red bars = p-value region)', fontsize=14)
ax.set_xticks(k_values)
ax.legend(['P(observed)'], loc='upper left')
ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)

# Add legend for bar colors
from matplotlib.patches import Patch
legend_elements = [Patch(facecolor='coral', label='p-value region'),
                   Patch(facecolor='steelblue', label='Not in p-value')]
ax.legend(handles=legend_elements, loc='upper right')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
snippets.expected_output: Bar chart with p-value region highlighted in red

snippets.title: Finding Critical Values
snippets.description: Determine the rejection region boundary (critical value)
snippets.code:
# Finding Critical Values for Hypothesis Testing
n = 20
p_null = 0.40
alpha = 0.05

print(f"Finding Critical Value for One-Tailed Test (Upper)")
print(f"H₀: p ≤ {p_null} vs Hₐ: p > {p_null}")
print(f"n = {n}, α = {alpha}")
print("="*50)

# Calculate P(X ≥ k) for each k
print("\nk\tP(X ≥ k)\tReject H₀?")
print("-"*40)

critical_value = None
for k in range(0, n + 1):
    tail_prob = 1 - binom.cdf(k - 1, n, p_null)  # P(X >= k) = 1 - P(X <= k-1)
    reject = "YES" if tail_prob < alpha else "NO"
    if tail_prob < alpha and critical_value is None:
        critical_value = k
        print(f"{k}\t{tail_prob:.5f}\t\t{reject} ← Critical value")
    elif k >= 8 and k <= 15:  # Show relevant range
        print(f"{k}\t{tail_prob:.5f}\t\t{reject}")

print(f"\nCritical value k* = {critical_value}")
print(f"Decision rule: Reject H₀ if observed successes ≥ {critical_value}")

# Verify with binomtest
print(f"\nVerification:")
result_at_critical = binomtest(k=critical_value, n=n, p=p_null, alternative='greater')
result_below_critical = binomtest(k=critical_value - 1, n=n, p=p_null, alternative='greater')
print(f"P(X ≥ {critical_value}) = {result_at_critical.pvalue:.5f} < {alpha} ✓")
print(f"P(X ≥ {critical_value - 1}) = {result_below_critical.pvalue:.5f} > {alpha} ✓")
snippets.expected_output: Table showing critical value determination process

snippets.title: Comparing One-Tailed vs Two-Tailed Tests
snippets.description: Visualize the difference between test types
snippets.code:
# Compare One-Tailed vs Two-Tailed Tests
n = 20
p_null = 0.5
observed = 14

k_values = np.arange(0, n + 1)
probabilities = binom.pmf(k_values, n, p_null)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))

# One-tailed test (upper)
ax1 = axes[0]
colors_one = ['coral' if k >= observed else 'steelblue' for k in k_values]
ax1.bar(k_values, probabilities, color=colors_one, edgecolor='black', alpha=0.7)
p_value_one = binomtest(k=observed, n=n, p=p_null, alternative='greater').pvalue
ax1.set_title(f'One-Tailed Test (Hₐ: p > 0.5)\np-value = {p_value_one:.4f}', fontsize=12)
ax1.set_xlabel('k')
ax1.set_ylabel('P(X = k)')
ax1.axvline(x=n*p_null, color='green', linestyle='--', label=f'Mean = {n*p_null}')
ax1.legend()

# Two-tailed test
ax2 = axes[1]
# For symmetric distribution, values as extreme as 14 are 14, 15, ..., 20 and 0, 1, ..., 6
deviation = abs(observed - n*p_null)  # 14 - 10 = 4
lower_extreme = int(n*p_null - deviation)  # 10 - 4 = 6
colors_two = ['coral' if (k >= observed or k <= lower_extreme) else 'steelblue' for k in k_values]
ax2.bar(k_values, probabilities, color=colors_two, edgecolor='black', alpha=0.7)
p_value_two = binomtest(k=observed, n=n, p=p_null, alternative='two-sided').pvalue
ax2.set_title(f'Two-Tailed Test (Hₐ: p ≠ 0.5)\np-value = {p_value_two:.4f}', fontsize=12)
ax2.set_xlabel('k')
ax2.set_ylabel('P(X = k)')
ax2.axvline(x=n*p_null, color='green', linestyle='--', label=f'Mean = {n*p_null}')
ax2.legend()

fig.suptitle(f'Observed: {observed} successes out of {n}', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print(f"Summary for observed = {observed}, n = {n}, p₀ = {p_null}:")
print(f"  One-tailed p-value: {p_value_one:.4f} → {'Reject' if p_value_one < 0.05 else 'Fail to reject'} at α = 0.05")
print(f"  Two-tailed p-value: {p_value_two:.4f} → {'Reject' if p_value_two < 0.05 else 'Fail to reject'} at α = 0.05")
snippets.expected_output: Side-by-side comparison of one-tailed and two-tailed test rejection regions

snippets.title: Disease Transmission Example - Complete Analysis
snippets.description: Full hypothesis test for virus transmissibility claim
snippets.code:
# Complete Analysis: Is the New Variant More Transmissible?
print("="*70)
print("HYPOTHESIS TEST: New Virus Variant Transmissibility")
print("="*70)

# Parameters
n = 300  # Contact cases examined
observed_infections = 190
p_old = 0.60  # Old variant transmission rate

print(f"\nData:")
print(f"  - Contact cases examined: n = {n}")
print(f"  - Infections observed: {observed_infections}")
print(f"  - Observed transmission rate: {observed_infections/n:.4f}")
print(f"  - Old variant rate: p = {p_old}")

# Step 1: State hypotheses
print(f"\n--- Step 1: State Hypotheses ---")
print(f"H₀: p ≤ {p_old} (new variant is NOT more transmissible)")
print(f"Hₐ: p > {p_old} (new variant IS more transmissible)")

# Step 2: Calculate test statistic
print(f"\n--- Step 2: Test Statistic ---")
p_hat = observed_infections / n
print(f"Sample proportion: p̂ = {observed_infections}/{n} = {p_hat:.4f}")

# Step 3: Calculate p-value
print(f"\n--- Step 3: Calculate p-value ---")
result = binomtest(k=observed_infections, n=n, p=p_old, alternative='greater')
print(f"p-value = P(X ≥ {observed_infections} | p = {p_old}) = {result.pvalue:.6f}")

# Step 4: Decision
print(f"\n--- Step 4: Decision (α = 0.05) ---")
alpha = 0.05
if result.pvalue < alpha:
    print(f"Since p-value = {result.pvalue:.6f} < α = {alpha}")
    print(f"Decision: REJECT H₀")
    print(f"\nConclusion: There is statistically significant evidence")
    print(f"that the new variant has higher transmissibility than {p_old*100}%.")
else:
    print(f"Since p-value = {result.pvalue:.6f} ≥ α = {alpha}")
    print(f"Decision: FAIL TO REJECT H₀")
    print(f"\nConclusion: Insufficient evidence to conclude")
    print(f"that the new variant is more transmissible.")

# Visualization
print(f"\n--- Visualization ---")
k_values = np.arange(150, 220)
probabilities = binom.pmf(k_values, n, p_old)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 5))
colors = ['coral' if k >= observed_infections else 'steelblue' for k in k_values]
ax.bar(k_values, probabilities, color=colors, edgecolor='none', alpha=0.7)
ax.axvline(x=observed_infections, color='red', linestyle='--', linewidth=2, label=f'Observed = {observed_infections}')
ax.axvline(x=n*p_old, color='green', linestyle='-', linewidth=2, label=f'Expected = {n*p_old}')
ax.set_xlabel('Number of Infections (k)', fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel('P(X = k) under H₀', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title(f'Binomial Distribution under H₀: p = {p_old}\np-value = P(X ≥ {observed_infections}) = {result.pvalue:.4f}', fontsize=14)
ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
snippets.expected_output: Complete hypothesis test with visualization for disease transmission
